scope: Direct-call quick reference for the currently confirmed Tencent Video API capability recipes, optimized for callers who want the shortest stable path first within the currently tested anonymous direct-call scope.
caution: This file summarizes the current best direct-call recipes in the tested anonymous direct-call scope plus the later real anonymous visitor-cookie replay where explicitly noted.
It is not proof that aged-cookie or login-state environments behave the same.

These recipes prioritize the shortest stable path in the currently tested anonymous direct-call scope; they are not automatically the globally safest path across all cookie ages, login states, or wider sample space.
When a recipe is marked branch_partially_closed or environment_open, keep the canonical branch unless you are deliberately probing that branch family.

recipes.name: api1_single_cover_lookup
expected_highlights: top-level errorno=0
non-empty cover title
non-empty video_ids family
recipes.current_best_reading: The most stable starting point when you have a CID and want the cover shell plus its video-id family.

recipes.name: api1_single_cover_lookup_tid537_probe_shell
recipes.intent: CID -> explicit API1 tid=537 success shell probe
expected_highlights: errorno=0 on confirmed samples
shell-level success without video_ids on the current public matrix
do not assume canonical tid=431 cover richness
recipes.current_best_reading: A separate success-shell-without-sample branch. Use it only when you intentionally need to probe tid=537; it is not a canonical replacement for tid=431.
recipes.environment_scope: same-day anonymous tested branch plus the current 3-CID caller-facing richness matrix

recipes.name: api1_single_cover_lookup_tid453_cover_shell_only
recipes.intent: CID -> explicit API1 tid=453 cover-only positive shell
expected_highlights: errorno=0 on confirmed samples
non-empty cover title/type with video_ids_count=0 on the current public matrix
do not assume canonical tid=431 video_ids richness
recipes.current_best_reading: A separate positive cover-shell-only branch. Use it only when you intentionally need to probe tid=453; it is richer than tid=537 but still not a canonical replacement for tid=431.
recipes.environment_scope: same-day anonymous tested branch plus the focused 3-CID confirmation on the new 453 branch

recipes.name: api1_single_cover_lookup_tid483_video_ids_led_thin_shell
recipes.intent: CID -> explicit API1 tid=483 video_ids-led thin shell
expected_highlights: errorno=0 on confirmed samples
cover title/type/pay_status stay empty while video_ids_count>=1 on the current public matrix
do not assume canonical tid=431 cover richness even if downstream API2 detail chaining still works in the demos
recipes.current_best_reading: A separate video_ids-led thin success shell. Use it only when you intentionally need tid=483; it can still feed downstream canonical API2 detail chaining in the repository demos, but it is not a field-equivalent replacement for tid=431.
recipes.environment_scope: same-day anonymous tested branch plus the focused 3-CID confirmation and dedicated Python/Go plus raw HTTP live validation on the primary public sample

recipes.name: api1_batch_cover_lookup
recipes.intent: CID batch -> multiple cover shells in one request
expected_highlights: duplicates are preserved
illegal CID can coexist with top-level success
empty slots are ignored
recipes.current_best_reading: The public demos now expose a formal multi-CID entrypoint and return cover_infos plus api1_batch_diagnostics, but raw API1 calls remain clearer when you need parser-level batch-contract probing.

recipes.name: api2_single_detail_xml_canonical
recipes.intent: VID -> full-detail row on the canonical positive branch
expected_highlights: top-level errorno=0
non-empty title and duration
defn/state/upload_src available on canonical samples
recipes.current_best_reading: The best default recipe when callers expect the richest currently confirmed detail row.

recipes.name: api2_single_detail_xml_tid488_alt_positive_shell
recipes.intent: VID -> thinner alternate positive shell on tid=488
expected_highlights: retcode=0 on confirmed sample
title + url present
vid/duration/cover_list/create_time currently absent on the dedicated live-validation sample
recipes.current_best_reading: A real alternate positive branch, but currently the thinnest validated caller-facing shell in this family. Keep reading it as title+url-level success, not as a canonical-detail substitute and not as a richer-shell substitute for 502.
recipes.environment_scope: same-day anonymous tested branch with dedicated Python/Go plus raw HTTP live validation on the primary public sample

recipes.name: api2_single_detail_xml_tid502_alt_positive_shell
recipes.intent: VID -> richer alternate positive shell on tid=502
expected_highlights: retcode=0 on confirmed sample
title + url + vid + duration present
cover_list + create_time currently present on the dedicated live-validation sample
recipes.current_best_reading: A real alternate positive branch and currently the richest validated caller-facing shell below canonical 535. Keep reading it as stronger than 488/540/541, but still not as a field-equivalent replacement for 535.
recipes.environment_scope: same-day anonymous tested branch with dedicated Python/Go plus raw HTTP live validation on the primary public sample

recipes.name: api2_single_detail_xml_tid506_near_empty_success_shell
recipes.intent: VID -> API2 tid=506 near-empty success shell probe
expected_highlights: retcode=0 on confirmed sample
caller-facing row stays empty_shell=true on the dedicated demo and raw XML validation sample
do not assume canonical 535 detail richness even though top-level success still returns 0
recipes.current_best_reading: A stable near-empty success shell rather than a positive-detail recipe. Use it only when you intentionally need to probe tid=506; the current dedicated Python/Go and raw XML validation confirms top-level success with an effectively empty caller-facing row.
recipes.environment_scope: same-day anonymous tested branch with dedicated Python/Go plus raw XML live validation on the primary public sample

recipes.name: api2_single_detail_xml_tid540_alt_positive_shell
recipes.intent: VID -> alternate positive shell on tid=540
expected_highlights: retcode=0 on confirmed samples
positive row may be thinner than tid=535
do not assume canonical defn/state/upload_src richness
recipes.current_best_reading: A real positive branch, but keep reading it as an alternate positive shell rather than a field-equivalent replacement for 535. Current Python/Go parity evidence only strengthens that shell-level reading; it does not upgrade 540 into a canonical-detail substitute.

recipes.name: api2_single_detail_xml_tid541_alt_positive_shell
recipes.intent: VID -> alternate positive shell on tid=541
expected_highlights: retcode=0 on confirmed samples
positive row may be thinner than tid=535
do not assume canonical defn/state/upload_src richness
recipes.current_best_reading: Same family as 535/540, but still an alternate positive shell rather than a canonical replacement for 535. Current Python/Go parity evidence only strengthens that shell-level reading; it does not upgrade 541 into a canonical-detail substitute.

recipes.name: api2_single_detail_jsonp
expected_highlights: wrapper defaults to QZOutputJson=...;
detail semantics follow the same branch family as XML
recipes.current_best_reading: Use only exact lowercase json. Callback override is supported, empty callback falls back to QZOutputJson, `[` / `[[` still raw-passthrough, and the current parse-breaking representative family includes `a(` / `((` / `})();` plus the widened unmatched-delimiter additions `[(` / `{(` / `/*(` / `a[(` / `)(` / `](` / `}(`.

recipes.name: api2_batch_lookup
recipes.intent: VID batch -> multiple detail rows in one request
expected_highlights: mixed valid+invalid rows can coexist with top-level success
duplicates are preserved
33 non-empty items hit the over-limit error
recipes.current_best_reading: The clean positive upper bound is 32 non-empty items per request. Use caller-side batch diagnostics instead of trusting top-level success alone.
recipes.environment_scope: same-day anonymous canonical branch; JSONP mirror semantics also replay-stable in the tested anonymous real-cookie environment

recipes.name: api2_all_invalid_jsonp_batch_consumer_rule
recipes.intent: Recognize the all-invalid JSONP batch shell correctly on the caller side

preferred_path.curl: Send otype=json with an all-invalid idlist batch and inspect caller-side row shells rather than only top-level success

expected_highlights: top-level success can coexist with all rows empty_shell=true
caller must treat this as an all-invalid batch
recipes.current_best_reading: The stable consumer rule is: top-level success plus every row empty_shell=true means an all-invalid/empty-shell batch.

recipes.name: real_session_replay_template
recipes.intent: Replay the canonical contract matrix under a real browser-cookie environment
expected_highlights: replay the canonical API1/API2 contract cases under a real cookie environment
compare against the current anonymous baseline
recipes.current_best_reading: Tooling is ready, but the high-value remaining evidence still depends on receiving real aged-cookie or login-state header material.
recipes.environment_scope: anonymous real-cookie replay already landed; aged-cookie and login-state still waiting for inputs

recipes.name: replay_bundle_full_semantics_with_callback_tail
recipes.intent: One-command replay orchestration for matrix plus semantics probes

preferred_path.artifact_dirs: --artifact-output-dir and --subprocess-output-dir are the safest way to preserve outputs in constrained local environments

expected_highlights: run contract replay and semantics replay together
carry callback pathological-tail probes into the same replay bundle
recipes.current_best_reading: The callback-tail replay path is no longer only self-validated: a real anonymous-cookie full-semantics replay now completes successfully, and it still shows no new parameter branch beyond the current anonymous baseline. Promotion beyond anonymous scope still waits on aged-cookie/login-state inputs.
recipes.environment_scope: completed on the anonymous real-cookie replay env; intended next for aged/login
